title: making a website and being performative.
content: I created this website because I think having a website is cool. I don't really care too much for having a blog but I realised I can just put things on this website that I wouldn't put anywhere else on the internet because I think its cringe. For example, you will never see me with a substack or trying to make real posts on twitter. I just think its lame, and I'd also never market any of my music on social media. but hosting it on my own website is pretty chill and I don't mind. I had a problem whilst making it however, what would a oscar website even look like? what would I call it? This is the end result I'll probably make a post talking about why "Oscar Indigo" which I think is pretty cringe tbf. Hopefully later posts are more like well written, this one is just written because I needed a post before I sent the new bargelo link because I figured someone was genius enough to put blog. before the site.  Why are there naked girls on your screen? Well the answer has nothing to do with naked women, when I started to brainstorm ideas for what the website could look like. I came back to my love for early twentieth century aesthetics. The naked girls are all Jean-Gabriel Domergue paintings. When I thought of what the site could look like, I thought of what other people had done, the majority of those being attempts at reviving 90s nostalgia or early 2000s. I'm not really a gamer, I'm not really a internet guy or into cool internet shits. My interests are far more pretentious and less cool. If I were to make it look like windows95 I would just be larping like crazy. Having naked french girls is really my attempt at unperformativeness, I think it's uncool. I think most peoples initial reactions in 2026 is wow why has this guy got porn in the background. Even the non nudes, perpetuate the idealisation of femininity and the female body. The Male Gaze is something people are all aware of nowadays, I'm like male so you can think that I'm just evil and pushing my male gaze values. but to be honest I don't really believe too hard in the male gaze in art. I think its fine that I like to look at these idealised figures, I don't really think its attached to some crazy sexual centering or anything, I just think it looks nice.
